scene.title: The Pyramid's Foot
scene.intro: Word comes back from {party}: they have reached the nameless pyramid in the ash-flats, a black tooth of stone older than the Burning. Its one door is sealed with a rune that drinks the light. They send a runner for your word — force it, or find the patient way?

advisors.text: A door is only a wall that gave up once. Tell them to break it, Archon — we did not send them to admire the masonry.
Old seals were set by someone. Tell them to look for the maker's own way in, before they wake whatever the seal was keeping.

choices.label: Send word: break the seal with power

win.text: They pour the working into the rune until it cracks, and the black door grinds inward on a breath of very old air.
win.saga: {party} broke the light-drinking seal of the nameless pyramid with sheer power, and walked into the dark beneath it.

lose.text: The rune drinks the working whole and gives nothing back. Worse — something deeper in the stone answers, and the party pulls back to regroup.
lose.saga: {party} tried to break the pyramid's seal by force; it drank their working, and something within stirred.

choices.label: Send word: find the maker's hidden way

win.text: Days of cold searching, and Nyx's runner reports a servants' stair beneath a fallen slab. They go down quietly, the seal untouched.
win.saga: {party} found the maker's own hidden stair into the nameless pyramid, and went down without waking the seal.

scene.title: The Antechamber of Names
scene.intro: The party is deep now, and their season's word is uneasy. They have come to a hall whose walls are carved floor to vault with names — thousands of them, in a script that hurts to read. Some of the names, {party} swear, are of the living. One of yours is on the wall.

advisors.text: A hall of names in a forgetting world is not decoration, Archon. It is a ledger. Tell them to copy what they can and touch nothing.
If our name is written here, this place has a claim on us. Tell them to leave an offering, and go carefully to the heart.

choices.label: Have them copy the ledger of names

win.text: They fill three books before their lamps gutter. Whatever this pyramid was, it kept a record of the world's memory — and now, in part, so do you.
win.saga: In the pyramid's antechamber {party} copied a great ledger of names — a record of the world's memory, salvaged for Runehold.

lose.text: The script writhes as they copy it, and two of the books come away blank. They press on toward the heart, unsettled and a little less certain of their own names.
lose.saga: {party} tried to copy the pyramid's ledger of names, but the script would not hold to the page.

choices.label: Press straight for the heart of the pyramid

win.text: They leave the names unread and go down toward the heart, quicker but blinder for it.
win.saga: {party} pressed past the pyramid's hall of names, leaving its ledger unread, and went down toward the heart.

scene.title: The Heart of the Pyramid
scene.intro: At the pyramid's heart, {party} find a chamber holding a single object: a rune-cut stone that hums like a struck bell and remembers, they say, everything. It could be carried home — or they could work it here, where its power is anchored, and risk waking the pyramid fully.

advisors.text: Take it and go, Archon. A bird in the hand outlives a working in a haunted vault.
Anchored power is power doubled. Tell them to use it here, once, before it is ours to keep — we may never stand in such a place again.

win.text: They wrap the stone and climb for daylight. It sings all the way home, and Runehold is richer in memory and wonder for it.
win.saga: {party} bore the singing memory-stone home from the heart of the nameless pyramid — a treasure of old memory for Runehold.

choices.label: Work the stone here, where it is anchored

win.text: They draw on the anchored stone and it answers past all hope — a season's forgotten workings come flooding back into their hands. They carry both the knowing and the stone up into the light.
win.saga: At the pyramid's heart {party} worked the anchored memory-stone and drew back a flood of forgotten magic — then bore the stone home besides.

lose.text: The stone gives more than they can hold. The pyramid wakes around them, the stair folds shut, and the dark takes the party with the working half-done.
lose.saga: {party} reached too deep into the pyramid's anchored stone; it woke the tomb around them, and they did not climb back into the light.
